token_cost.note: Estimated from word count (words * 1.35). Response-only; prompt side not counted.

efficiency.interpretation: With-skill responses are ~2.0x more verbose per response; on a per-rubric-point basis the multiplier narrows to ~1.8x because the with-skill responses also score higher. Clarity is the only criterion where without-skill wins (brevity advantage). Correctness nearly ties at ceiling because the no-skill baseline handles these questions well given the model's general knowledge — the real separation is on completeness and actionability.

rationale: Total delta +3.00 / 25, driven by completeness (+1.75), structure (+1.00), and actionability (+1.00); correctness barely separates (+0.25) because both conditions recover the main facts. Clarity is -1.00 against the skill due to ~2x verbosity. The skill's structured output format (claim-type audit, consensus/nuances/contradictions blocks, explicit confidence tiers, steelman of alternatives) adds defensible rigor that is valuable specifically when the user needs to act on contested claims or relay the reasoning to others — the modal trigger for this skill. For casual one-shot research, the without-skill responses are correct, tight, and faster to read, so the cost is real. Recommend activating for contested / multi-source / decision-support queries; the ~880 extra tokens per response is justified by the completeness and actionability gains, which are the criteria that most translate to user-facing value for this skill.
